commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an agent-core guard that emits `agent_end` after `turn_end` when a tool aborts the active run, plus a regression test that fails if the model is called again after that abort.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +53. Total +57 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main can continue past `turn_end` into another provider-turn decision after a tool aborts the run signal. I did not run the regression test because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof, not a concrete code repair for ClawSweeper to make.

target.title: fix(agent-core): stop loop after aborted tool run
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop the agent loop after an in-flight tool aborts the run, once the existing tool result and turn_end have been emitted.
- Avoid requesting another model turn with an already-aborted signal.
- Add a regression test that fails if a tool-triggered abort calls the model a second time.

## Real behavior proof
- Reproduced against an OpenClaw 2026.6.6 gateway: aborting during an `exec sleep 30` tool call returned quickly and wrote the SIGTERM tool result, but the next send on the same session waited about 60s.
- Gateway logs showed `lane wait exceeded` and `session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released` after the abort path attempted another provider turn with an aborted signal.
- The new test failed before the fix with `model was called after abort` and passes after the change.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:packages`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
target.author: szsip239
